goal: Obtain a practical driving license within about three months
constraints: Plan must adapt to local licensing rules (permits, minimum practice hours, test booking windows)
Budget constraint: budget for lessons is about $800
Include at least one professional mock with an instructor near the test date
Budget allocation: maintain total budget around $800 with focus on lessons; mocks kept within this limit
Budget planning: 8–12 lessons may be needed depending on local hourly rates; keep total cost near $800

Target total driving hours before the practical test: ~40 hours, comprising 8–12 hours with a professional instructor and 20–30 hours of supervised family practice; progress assessed by readiness markers.
decisions: Adopt hazard perception practice apps aligned with local test format
Maintain a Google Sheets fault log for tracking faults and progress
Book one or two paid instructor mock tests to get objective scoring
Produce printable one-page visor checklist for quick pre-drive car checks
Adopt cost-efficient weekly practice schedule: 1x60-minute instructor lesson per week, 1x30–60 minute family practice with dad, 1x60–120 minute weekend session
Start with an initial block of 6–8 lessons to lock in clutch/hill starts and basic control, then 2–3 lessons for junctions/parking and higher-speed roads
Reserve 1–2 lessons near the end as a full mock test and pre-test tune-up
Reassess with instructor after the initial block to determine if additional lessons are needed
Evaluate the hours-to-cost based on local hourly rate to stay within budget
Emphasise parallel parking, motorway merges/lanes and observation/shoulder checks in mock feedback
entities: hazard perception apps used for practice
midweek 30–60 minute family practice session with dad
detailed readiness markers for test readiness (clutch control, hill starts, observation, junctions, parking, motorway merges)
licence entitlement implications for manual vs automatic
motorway exposure schedule (dual carriageways, slip roads, motorway)
parallel parking cue phrases for busy streets
parallel parking progression drills (cones → quiet streets → real cars → busy streets with instructor)
video review of parallel parking practice
dad coaching guidelines for parallel parking practice
mock feedback focus areas: parallel parking, motorway merges/lanes, observation/shoulder checks
